title: Vangueriella orthacantha
taxonomy: Vangueriella orthacantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is part of the genus Vangueriella, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily native to tropical regions of Africa. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, often referred to as the madder family. It is comprised of over 600 genera and 13,000 known species, many of which are woody plants. The classification of Vangueriella orthacantha reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Vangueriella orthacantha, also known as the 'bush mango', is a shrub or small tree native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant is prized for its health benefits, including the potential to treat fever, malaria, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Vangueriella orthacantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in the production of herbal remedies such as tonics and salves.
